name: Docusign
price_basis: Personal eSignature at $11 per month as a $132 annual commitment, checked 2026-07
verdict_reason: KEEP: a signature has to stand up in court. Legal standing is a wall no weekend build climbs over.
what_zo_builds: The filing system around the signatures: Zo archives every completed envelope into your workspace with parties, dates, and a searchable index, tracks envelope usage against the Personal plan's five per month, and flags renewal time with real numbers. What it does not do is pretend a homemade signing page carries the same weight with a counterparty's lawyers.
dont_kill_if: The one honest exit: you sign fewer than five low-stakes documents a year and your counterparties accept simpler tools. Then free tiers cover you and Personal is rent.
build_brief: Goal: keep Docusign for legal standing but own my agreement archive. Pull every completed envelope with its certificate of completion into /contracts, indexed by party, date, and renewal terms. Track envelope usage against the five-per-month Personal allowance and flag months that approach it. Add a yearly review that lists what was signed and what the plan cost. Done when every agreement lives in my archive with its audit certificate and the renewal decision has usage data behind it.
